auto_approve.mechanism: mcp_per_tool_approval: Codex supports per-tool approval configuration to suppress confirmation prompts for trusted tools

enterprise_management.mechanism: Codex does not document organization-level MCP management

env_var_expansion.mechanism: Codex MCP configuration does not document environment variable expansion

marketplace.mechanism: Codex does not have an in-IDE MCP marketplace

oauth_support.mechanism: mcp_oauth_support: Codex supports OAuth 2.0 authentication for remote MCP servers

resource_referencing.mechanism: Codex does not document MCP resource referencing

tool_filtering.mechanism: mcp_enabled_disabled_tools: Codex supports per-server tool enable/disable configuration controlling which tools are exposed to the agent

transport_types.mechanism: Codex MCP transport types not documented beyond basic MCP support; likely stdio only

providerExtensions.name: MCP Servers Config (config.toml mcp_servers)
providerExtensions.summary: MCP servers declared under mcp_servers in config.toml as RawMcpServerConfig entries (command/url, args, env, headers, OAuth, timeouts, enabled/disabled tools).

providerExtensions.name: Per-Tool Approval Mode
providerExtensions.summary: Each MCP server can set per-tool approval_mode via the tools map, overriding session-level approval policy for individual tools.

providerExtensions.name: MCP OAuth Support
providerExtensions.summary: OAuth fields mcp_oauth_callback_port, mcp_oauth_callback_url, and mcp_oauth_credentials_store (keyring/file/auto), plus per-server oauth_resource and scopes.

providerExtensions.name: Codex MCP Server Interface (codex mcp-server)
providerExtensions.summary: Codex exposes itself as an MCP server via `codex mcp-server` (JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio) for thread, turn, config, and event-stream control.

providerExtensions.name: MCP Tool Allowlist and Blocklist
providerExtensions.summary: Per-server enabled_tools (allowlist) and disabled_tools (blocklist) arrays restrict which tools an MCP server exposes.
